LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO DEEM ADMITTED THE ENTIRETY OF SET ONE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS SERVED ON PLAINTIFF CRISTINA SANCHEZ IS GRANTED. VOLKSWAGEN HAS SHOWN THAT CRISTINA SANCHEZ HAS NOT RESPONDED TO THE SET ONE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS AND THUS ALL OF THOSE REQUESTS ARE DEEMED ADMITTED. DUE TO HER FAILURE TO RESPOND TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS OR THIS MOTION, NO LATER THAN APRIL 17, 2026 CRISTINA SANCHEZ MUST PAY REASONABLE MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $455 TO VOLKSWAGEN. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. VISITING JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/HEK)

DISCOVERY 301, DEFENDANT VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF CRISTINA SANCHEZ TO RESPOND TO SET ONE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION IS GRANTED. VOLKSWAGEN HAS SHOWN THAT CRISTINA SANCHEZ HAS NOT RESPONDED TO THE SET ONE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION. NO LATER THAN MARCH 25, 2026 CRISTINA SANCHEZ MUST SERVE VERIFIED CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTIONS TO ALL SET ONE DOCUMENT REQUESTS AND PRODUCE ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS. DUE TO HER FAILURE TO RESPOND TO THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OR THIS MOTION, NO LATER THAN APRIL 17, 2026 CRISTINA SANCHEZ MUST PAY REASONABLE MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $455 TO VOLKSWAGEN. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. VISITING JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/HEK)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Cristina Gabriela Sanchez?S Initial Responses To Requests For Production, Set One; And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel Of Record
Off Calendar Mar-05-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff Cristina Gabriela Sanchez?S Initial Responses To Requests For Production, Set One; And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel Of Record.

Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.'s motion to compel plaintiff Cristina Sanchez to respond to set one requests for production is granted.

Volkswagen has shown that Cristina Sanchez has not responded to the set one requests for production. No later than March 25, 2026 Cristina Sanchez must serve verified code-compliant responses without objections to all set one document requests and produce all responsive documents. Due to her failure to respond to the requests for production or this motion, no later than April 17, 2026 Cristina Sanchez must pay reasonable monetary sanctions of $455 to Volkswagen.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)
